medical_history.chief_complaint: Intermittent abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, constipation and numbness of bilateral lower limb extremities, of 3 days duration
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Recurrent episodes of severe abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, constipation and numbness of bilateral lower limb extremities over 2 years, with six acute hospital admissions. Previous misdiagnoses include acute appendicitis, sinus tachycardia, renal calculi, drug-induced acute interstitial nephritis, and partial intestinal obstruction.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Non-diabetic, no previous history of hypertension. Usage of over-the-counter analgesics for 6 weeks. Previous surgeries: appendectomy and laparoscopy.
medical_history.family_history: Four out of five family members who underwent targeted mutation analyses were mutation-positive for the HMBS gene mutation.

physical_examination.general_examination: Thin built (BMI=20 kg/m2), pale. Brachial blood pressure was 160/90 mmHg. Scars of previous appendectomy and laparoscopy surgeries on the abdominal.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Muscle power was 4/5 in all four limbs (could not move against a good resistance).
